title: Canada gives share of bridge profits to US to get Trump nod
summary_full: The Gordie Howe International Bridge will open on July 27 connecting Detroit and Windsor. Canada agreed to share net toll profits with the United States after construction costs. This agreement resolves a long-standing dispute between the two trading partners. Canada funded the entire $4.5 billion construction project over the Detroit River. The deal directs proceeds to a US-run regional development fund.
